name: Athlete with overreaching markers — needs deload
description: Recent metrics show overreaching: HRV is well below baseline, sleep is poor, RHR elevated. Athlete is mid-block and wants to keep training, but the plan should pull volume back and prioritize recovery before resuming progression.
factsBlock:
Recent state (MUST RESPECT):
- HRV dropped from 7-day avg of 58 to 42 over the past 5 days (~28% drop).
- Sleep averaging 6.1h, well below the athlete's normal 7.5h.
- Athlete reports leg heaviness and unusually high RPE on easy runs.
- No race on the horizon — this is general fitness training.
must_have: Week 1 is a deload — total volume cut 30-40% vs typical training
At most ONE quality session in Week 1 (or zero — easy/recovery only is also valid)
Easy/recovery sessions dominate (>85% of total volume at low intensity)
Week 2 begins gradual reload only if recovery would have improved (still conservative — no return to prior peak volume)
Plan narrative or risks section explicitly references the recovery markers
must_not_have: Multiple quality sessions in Week 1 (VO2max + threshold + long run is wrong here)
Treating this like a normal training week
